source: Caramuel, Primus Calamus (1663), p.482 (scan idx 481), TAB XXXI
family: rebus-cancellatum (13x13 grid; ornamental capitals A/M/F as grille, interleaved with emblem-cells decoded by a numbered key)
title_diplomatic: (key row) 1 dicit perfectionem, 2 praemium, 3 pietatem, 4 ut ornatum, ADAMAN[tem], 5 AMETHystus, 6 alba fama, 7 super amoris ... (foot) 8 flos amoris et fecunditas estem clara
note: A pictorial rebus labyrinth: the recurring capitals A, M, F tile the lattice (likely AMor / Fama / Maria triads), while each EMBLEM (heart=amor, palm=victory, chalice, lamp=lumen, pyramid, eagle, amethyst) is a token decoded by the numbered legend. The verse is assembled by substituting emblem->word per the key and reading the grille letters. The most purely IMAGE-CODED plate.
confidence: mixed (grille letters + key high, emblem-to-cell mapping med)
calibration_role: Most purely PICTORIAL plate — the meaning lives in an emblem->word substitution key, the clearest case of a non-textual Language (the decode key IS a Language whose admits() maps emblems to lexemes). Tests that the engine treats a pictorial code as just another chart.
